title: Sorbus austriaca
taxonomy: Sorbus austriaca is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Rosaceae. This classification places it in the order Rosales and the subfamily Amygdaloideae. Further breaking it down, Sorbus austriaca can be classified as a species of the genus Sorbus. The genus Sorbus is a group of flowering plants commonly known as rowans. This classification is based on its physical characteristics, growth habits, and evolutionary relationships with other species within the Rosaceae family.

intro: Sorbus austriaca, also known as the Austrian whitebeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree species within the Rosaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Sorbus austriaca include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sorbus austriaca include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and infections.
